column1: # OBF -> DEOB per-class MEMBER map for the deob subsystem packages
# The reconcile phase applies this dictionary: given a drift call site `receiver.X(args)`
# where the receiver's DEOB type is known and X is the obf short-name (or a stale deob name),
# look up the row keyed by (deob_class, obf_member, arg_count_or_desc) -> deob_member.
#   deob_class          deob class that OWNS the member (receiver's deob type)
#   obf_member          obf member name as called at drift sites (single letter etc.);
#                       for stale-deob-name drifts the note carries "(stale: <oldname>)"
#   arg_count_or_desc   ''=field; for methods the JVM-ish param descriptor / arg list
#                       (disambiguates overloaded obf names like a/b/e). OBF arg count may
#                       differ from deob arg count where dummy/guard params were dropped.
#   deob_member         the correct deob member name as DECLARED in the owning class
#   note                provenance / drift kind / behavioral-bug flag / FLAG=manual-resolve
# GROUND TRUTH: each deob file's // obf comments + decompiled/normalized-clean/*.java
# (obf names + descriptors) + javap of rsclassic-1091943135.jar + docs/NAMING.md.
# obf class map: see docs/NAMING.md. NOTE obf `n` is a MERGED class -> split into
# Scanline (instance span fields e/k/d/l) + FontWidths (static globals).
# UNIMPORTED CROSS-PACKAGE ALIASES (type-level, not member drift; fix the import/qualifier):
#   client.shell.Mudclient / client.Mudclient  -> dangling refs to the EXCLUDED Mudclient class
#       (SurfaceSprite.java:5/45, Timer.java:3) — out of subsystem scope; resolve when Mudclient lands.
#   Scene/GameModel frustum globals: 6 statics live on obf classes m/da/oa/aa/nb (see frustum* rows);
#       Scene reads them by raw obf field, GameModel by deob name — must UNIFY (rows below give the names).
# BARE OBF-CLASS RECEIVERS (Scene render path + World/Panel/Utility static-alias sites): the call sites
# write `aa.b`/`wb.a`/`m.j`/etc. = an OBF CLASS used as a static receiver. Resolve owner via NAMING.md
# (aa=BZip, wb=MessageList, m=SocketFactory, da=ClientStream, oa=NameHash, nb=DataStore, gb=ProxySocketFactory,
#  jb=DownloadWorker, p=Timer, cb=CacheUpdater, ia=SpriteScaler, ua=Surface, t=EntityDef, ib=StreamBase,
#  e=GameShell, ba=SurfaceSprite, k=World, db=LinkedQueue, u=StringCodec) THEN rename the member via its row.
